text: When working with APIs, you need to understand how to send HTTP requests to get or send data. Most APIs return information in JSON format, which is easy to read and process. However, before you can access the data, you often need authentication. This means providing a key or token to prove you have permission. APIs also have rate limits, which control how many requests you can make in a certain time period. If you exceed the limit, the API may temporarily block you. Finally, you should include error handling in your code. This helps you deal with problems like failed requests or invalid data without crashing your program.
